2604.26676.title: A Toolkit for Detecting Spurious Correlations in Speech Datasets
2604.26676.paper_summary: We introduce a toolkit for uncovering spurious correlations between recording characteristics and target class in speech datasets. Spurious correlations may arise due to heterogeneous recording conditions, a common scenario for health-related datasets. When present both in the training and test data, these correlations result in an overestimation of the system performance -- a dangerous situation, specially in high-stakes application where systems are required to satisfy minimum performance requirements. Our toolkit implements a diagnostic method based on the detection of the target class using only the non-speech regions in the audio. Better than chance performance at this task indicates that information about the target class can be extracted from the non-speech regions, flagging the presence of spurious correlations. The toolkit is publicly available for research use.
2604.26676.paper_summary_zh: 我們介紹了一個工具包，用於揭示語音數據集中錄音特徵與目標類別之間的虛假相關性。虛假相關性可能由於異質的錄音條件而產生，這在健康相關數據集中是一種常見情況。當這些相關性同時存在於訓練數據和測試數據中時，會導致系統性能的高估——這是一個危險的情況，特別是在需要滿足最低性能要求的高風險應用中。我們的工具包實現了一種基於僅使用音頻中的非語音區域來檢測目標類別的診斷方法。在這項任務中，表現優於隨機機率表明可以從非語音區域提取有關目標類別的信息，標誌著虛假相關性的存在。該工具包已公開供研究使用。
2604.26676.author: Lara Gauder et.al.
2604.26676.authors: Lara Gauder, Pablo Riera, Andrea Slachevsky, Gonzalo Forno, Adolfo M. García, Luciana Ferrer
